campus: Rome (Department of Computer, Control and Management Engineering 'Antonio Ruberti' - DIAG; Faculty of Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics)
degree_class: LM-DATA (Data Science; formerly delivered under LM-91)
faculty: Faculty of Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics
admission_test_type: Verification of curricular requirements + appropriate personal preparation (APP); verification interview if curricular/English requirements not documented
application_platform: MoveIN platform (sapienza.gomovein.com) for non-EU pre-selection; Infostud for admission/enrolment; programme info at datascience.i3s.uniroma1.it
access_quota_non_eu: Maximum 25 applicants per individual non-EU country (general Sapienza MoveIN rule); programme-specific non-EU contingent per the call [uncertain]
places_and_foreign_contingent: Programme places and non-EU contingent set in the official call; exact 2026-2027 figures not extracted [uncertain]

academic_requirements.prior_degree: A three-year bachelor's degree or university diploma, or other adequate qualification obtained abroad.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: At least 90 ECTS/CFU in the specified set of Academic Disciplines (SSD). The curricular requirements are designed to give access to graduates of classes L-8 (Information Engineering), L-31 (Computer Science) and L-41 (Statistics).
academic_requirements.personal_preparation: Appropriate personal preparation (APP); a verification interview is held for candidates lacking the required credits in specific sectors.

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign first-level degrees must be recognized as adequate/comparable per Sapienza/MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027. Specific country rules not individually extracted [uncertain]
curricular_integration_policy: Candidates lacking required credits in specific SSDs must undergo a verification interview; curricular gaps may need to be filled before admission per programme regulations [uncertain]
conditional_enrolment_policy: Applicants in the final year of their bachelor's may apply with enrolment conditional on completing the degree by the enrolment deadline [uncertain]

language_requirements.note: Students without a B2 English certificate, or who did not achieve at least 3 credits in English exams (including qualifying exams without a grade), must attend an interview to verify their English knowledge. The programme is taught entirely in English.

language_submission_timing: At MoveIN pre-selection (non-EU) and/or at admission; English verified by interview if not documented
italian_language_requirement: Italian not required for admission; programme fully in English.
application_deadline_eu: Open until 31 July 2026 for EU and EU-equivalent candidates (A.Y. 2026-2027). [uncertain - programme-internal admission deadlines may differ]
application_deadline_non_eu: MoveIN pre-selection window 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026 for non-EU candidates residing abroad requiring a visa (A.Y. 2026-2027).
application_fee: €30 per application via MoveIN pre-selection (max 2 applications per candidate per intake)
visa_preenrolment_deadline: MoveIN pre-selection by 15 May 2026; subsequent visa pre-enrolment via Universitaly per national deadline
required_documents: Academic transcript with ECTS/CFU and SSD detail
English language certificate (B2) or evidence of English credits
For foreign qualifications: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement, legalized/translated documents
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents require official translation and legalization/apostille, plus a Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA comparability statement.

entry_exam_or_test.selection: Verification of curricular requirements (>=90 ECTS in required SSDs) and appropriate personal preparation; a verification interview is held where curricular or English requirements are not documented.

test_variant_and_session: Verification interviews scheduled per the faculty/programme calendar [uncertain]
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Admission by meeting curricular thresholds and positive APP assessment; no numeric public threshold [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: To be admitted to the masters degree programme you need to meet the curricular requirements (RC) and have an appropriate personal preparation (APP), including a 3-year degree or university diploma, or other adequate educational qualifications gained abroad.

The curricular requirements seek to ensure access to the degree programme for all the graduates in the degree classes L-8 (Information Technology Engineering), L-31 (Computer Science) and L-41 (Statistics).

Students without a level B2 English language certificate or who did not achieve at least 3 Credits in English exams (including qualifying exams without a grade), will have to attend an interview to have their English language knowledge verified.
A maximum of 25 applicants per individual non-EU country will be admitted.

uncertainty_notes: Degree class: programme renewed under the new class LM-DATA (Data Science); earlier cohorts were under LM-91. Confirm the exact 2026-2027 class label on the /course/33519 catalogue page.
Programme-specific places and non-EU contingent for 2026-2027 not extracted from the official call; only the general MoveIN rule (max 25 per non-EU country) confirmed.
Curricular threshold stated as 90 ECTS on the programme admission page; exact SSD codes not transcribed.
EU programme-internal admission deadline vs. general 31 July 2026 enrolment deadline may differ for restricted-access masters; confirm on the call.
WebFetch was disabled in this environment; values gathered via official-domain web search summaries rather than direct page parsing.
